file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env zsh
# Ultra-Optimized .zshrc for macOS with Zinit & Advanced Performance Features
# Profile with: zmodload zsh/zprof (at top) and zprof (at bottom)

# ============================================================================
# 1. EARLY INITIALIZATION & INSTANT PROMPT
# ============================================================================

# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt (if using instead of starship)
if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
  source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
fi

# Amazon Q pre block
[[ -f "${HOME}/Library/Application Support/amazon-q/shell/zshrc.pre.zsh" ]] && \
    builtin source "${HOME}/Library/Application Support/amazon-q/shell/zshrc.pre.zsh"

# Terminal settings
export BROWSER="open -a 'Arc'"
export TERM=screen-256color
export ZSH_DISABLE_COMPFIX=true
export CLICOLOR=1

# Disable all timeouts
unset TMOUT
export TIMEOUT=0
export COMMAND_TIMEOUT=0

# Certificates & Tools
export CURL_CA_BUNDLE=$HOME/.nix/certs/system-root.pem
export HF_HUB_ENABLE_HF_TRANSFER=1

# XDG Base Directory Specification
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}"
export XDG_CACHE_HOME="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}"
export XDG_DATA_HOME="${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"
export XDG_STATE_HOME="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}"

# ============================================================================
# 2. ZINIT INSTALLATION & CONFIGURATION (Replaces Oh My Zsh)
# ============================================================================

# Increase function nesting limit to prevent recursion errors
# Set to 1000 for Amazon Q autosuggestions compatibility
export FUNCNEST=1000

ZINIT_HOME="${XDG_DATA_HOME:-${HOME}/.local/share}/zinit/zinit.git"

# Download Zinit if not present
if [ ! -d "$ZINIT_HOME" ]; then
    print -P "%F{33} %F{220}Installing %F{33}ZDHARMA-CONTINUUM%F{220} Initiative Plugin Manager (%F{33}zdharma-continuum/zinit%F{220})…%f"
    command mkdir -p "$(dirname $ZINIT_HOME)"
    command git clone https://github.com/zdharma-continuum/zinit.git "$ZINIT_HOME" && \
        print -P "%F{33} %F{34}Installation successful.%f%b" || \
        print -P "%F{160} The clone has failed.%f%b"
fi

source "${ZINIT_HOME}/zinit.zsh"

# ============================================================================
# 3. PATH CONFIGURATION (Deduplicated & Optimized)
# ============================================================================

typeset -U path PATH  # Automatically removes duplicates

# Function to prepend to PATH safely
path_prepend() {
    path=("$1" $path)
}

# System & Package Managers
path_prepend "/opt/homebrew/bin"
path_prepend "/usr/local/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.local/bin"

# Development Tools
path_prepend "/usr/local/go/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.cargo/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.dotnet"
path_prepend "$HOME/.tfenv/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.pulumi/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.opencode/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.docker/bin"
path_prepend "${KREW_ROOT:-$HOME/.krew}/bin"

# Python Virtual Environments
path_prepend "$HOME/.venv/tools3/bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.venv/mlx/bin"

# Node.js
path_prepend "$HOME/node_modules/.bin"
path_prepend "$HOME/.cache/.bun/bin"

# PowerShell
path_prepend "/opt/microsoft/powershell/7"

# Android SDK
export ANDROID_SDK_ROOT="/opt/android/sdk"
export ANDROID_HOME="$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT"
path_prepend "${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/emulator"
path_prepend "${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platform-tools"
path_prepend "${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/tools/bin"

# Go Configuration
export GOROOT="/usr/local/go"
export GOPATH="/opt/go"
export GOBIN="$GOPATH/bin"
path_prepend "$GOBIN"

# .NET Configuration
export DOTNET_ROOT="$HOME/.dotnet"
export DOTNET_SDK="$DOTNET_ROOT/sdk/5.0.300"
export DOTNET_SDK_VERSION="5.0.300"

# Atuin
path_prepend "$HOME/.atuin/bin"

# ============================================================================
# 4. ZINIT PLUGINS (Fast & Efficient)
# ============================================================================

# Load async library first
zinit light mafredri/zsh-async

# Theme: Starship (Fast Rust-based prompt)
zinit ice as"command" from"gh-r" \
          atclone"./starship init zsh > init.zsh; ./starship completions zsh > _starship" \
          atpull"%atclone" src"init.zsh"
zinit light starship/starship

# Alternative: Powerlevel10k (if you prefer over starship)
# zinit ice depth=1; zinit light romkatv/powerlevel10k

# Essential plugins with turbo mode (lazy loading)
# DISABLED: zsh-autosuggestions to prevent conflict with Amazon Q
zinit wait lucid for \
    blockf \
        zsh-users/zsh-completions
# Commented out to use Amazon Q autosuggestions instead:
#    atload"!_zsh_autosuggest_start" \
#        zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions

# History search
zinit ice wait"0b" lucid atload'bindkey "^[[A" history-substring-search-up; bindkey "^[[B" history-substring-search-down'
zinit light zsh-users/zsh-history-substring-search

# Syntax highlighting - MUST load last to properly wrap all ZLE widgets
zinit ice wait"0c" lucid atinit"ZINIT[COMPINIT_OPTS]=-C; zicompinit; zicdreplay"
zinit light zdharma-continuum/fast-syntax-highlighting

# Git extras
zinit ice wait"0" lucid
zinit snippet OMZP::git

# Directory environment
zinit ice wait"0" lucid
zinit snippet OMZP::direnv

# Kubectl plugin
zinit ice wait"1" lucid
zinit snippet OMZP::kubectl

# Copy buffer plugin
zinit ice wait"1" lucid
zinit snippet OMZP::copybuffer

# FZF integration
zinit ice wait"0" lucid
zinit snippet OMZP::fzf

# Additional useful plugins
# Disabled autopair to fix recursion conflict with autosuggestions
# zinit ice wait"2" lucid
# zinit light hlissner/zsh-autopair

zinit ice wait"2" lucid
zinit light zdharma-continuum/history-search-multi-word

# ============================================================================
# 5. COMPLETION SYSTEM (Optimized with caching)
# ============================================================================

# Completion settings
zstyle ':completion:*' menu select
zstyle ':completion:*' matcher-list 'm:{a-z}={A-Z}'
zstyle ':completion:*' list-colors "${(s.:.)LS_COLORS}"
zstyle ':completion:*' group-name ''
zstyle ':completion:*' verbose true
zstyle ':completion:*' use-cache true
zstyle ':completion:*' cache-path "$XDG_CACHE_HOME/zsh/zcompcache"

# Initialize completions with caching
autoload -Uz compinit
() {
  local zcompdump="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/zsh/zcompdump-${ZSH_VERSION}"
  if [[ $zcompdump -nt /usr/share/zsh ]] && [[ ! $zcompdump.zwc -ot $zcompdump ]]; then
    compinit -C -d "$zcompdump"
  else
    compinit -i -d "$zcompdump"
    [[ -f "$zcompdump" && ! -f "$zcompdump.zwc" ]] && zcompile "$zcompdump"
  fi
}

# Bash completions support
autoload -U +X bashcompinit && bashcompinit

# ============================================================================
# 6. LAZY LOADING FOR HEAVY TOOLS
# ============================================================================

# NVM Lazy Loading (saves ~500ms)
export NVM_LAZY_LOAD=true
export NVM_COMPLETION=true
zinit ice wait"2" lucid
zinit light lukechilds/zsh-nvm

# Lazy load completions for heavy tools
make_completion_lazy() {
    local cmd=$1
    local completion_func="_${cmd}_lazy_completion"

    eval "
    ${completion_func}() {
        unfunction ${completion_func}
        zinit ice wait\"0\" lucid as\"completion\"
        zinit snippet <($cmd completion zsh)
        return 124
    }
    compdef ${completion_func} ${cmd}
    "
}

# Apply lazy loading to heavy completions
for cmd in docker kubectl helm argocd eksctl; do
    command -v $cmd &>/dev/null && make_completion_lazy $cmd
done

# ============================================================================
# 7. ASYNC BACKGROUND TASKS (Using zsh-async)
# ============================================================================

# Initialize async worker
async_init

# Load Azure CLI completions in background
load_azure_completions() {
    local cache_file="$XDG_CACHE_HOME/zsh/az_completions"
    if [[ ! -f "$cache_file" ]] || [[ "$HOME/.zsh/az.completion" -nt "$cache_file" ]]; then
        [[ -f "$HOME/.zsh/az.completion" ]] && cp "$HOME/.zsh/az.completion" "$cache_file"
    fi
    [[ -f "$cache_file" ]] && source "$cache_file"
}

# Start async job
async_start_worker azure_worker
async_register_callback azure_worker load_azure_completions
async_job azure_worker load_azure_completions

# Load Google Cloud SDK asynchronously
load_gcloud_sdk() {
    if [[ -f "$HOME/Downloads/google-cloud-sdk/path.zsh.inc" ]]; then
        source "$HOME/Downloads/google-cloud-sdk/path.zsh.inc"
        source "$HOME/Downloads/google-cloud-sdk/completion.zsh.inc" 2>/dev/null
    fi
}

async_job azure_worker load_gcloud_sdk

# ============================================================================
# 8. HISTORY CONFIGURATION (Optimized)
# ============================================================================

export HISTFILE="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/zsh/history"
export HISTSIZE=50000
export SAVEHIST=50000

setopt EXTENDED_HISTORY          # Write format: ": <beginning time>:<elapsed seconds>;<command>"
setopt HIST_EXPIRE_DUPS_FIRST    # Expire duplicate entries first
setopt HIST_IGNORE_DUPS          # Don't record duplicate entry
setopt HIST_IGNORE_SPACE         # Don't record entries starting with space
setopt HIST_VERIFY               # Show command before executing from history
setopt INC_APPEND_HISTORY        # Write to history immediately
setopt SHARE_HISTORY             # Share history between sessions
setopt HIST_REDUCE_BLANKS        # Remove extra blanks

# ============================================================================
# 9. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
# ============================================================================

# Editor configuration
export EDITOR=nvim
export VISUAL="$EDITOR"
export K9S_EDITOR="$EDITOR"

# Docker
export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
export COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1

# Azure
export ADAL_PYTHON_SSL_NO_VERIFY=1

# Pager & Colors
export LESS='-R -F -X'
export LESS_TERMCAP_mb=$'\e[1;31m'
export LESS_TERMCAP_md=$'\e[1;36m'
export LESS_TERMCAP_me=$'\e[0m'
export LESS_TERMCAP_se=$'\e[0m'
export LESS_TERMCAP_so=$'\e[01;44;33m'
export LESS_TERMCAP_ue=$'\e[0m'
export LESS_TERMCAP_us=$'\e[1;32m'

# ============================================================================
# 10. macOS-SPECIFIC OPTIMIZATIONS
# ============================================================================

if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
    # Use macOS Keychain for SSH
    export SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/System/Library/Services/ssh-keychain.xpc/Contents/MacOS/ssh-keychain.sock

    # Add TouchID for sudo (if not already configured)
    if ! grep -q "pam_tid.so" /etc/pam.d/sudo 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "# To enable TouchID for sudo, add this line to /etc/pam.d/sudo:"
        echo "# auth       sufficient     pam_tid.so"
    fi
fi

# GPG Configuration
export GPG_TTY=$(tty)
if command -v gpgconf &>/dev/null; then
    export SSH_AUTH_SOCK=$(gpgconf --list-dirs agent-ssh-socket 2>/dev/null)
    gpgconf --launch gpg-agent &>/dev/null
fi

# ============================================================================
# 11. FZF CONFIGURATION (Optimized)
# ============================================================================

export FZF_BASE="$HOME/.fzf"
export FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS="
  --height 40%
  --layout=reverse
  --border=sharp
  --inline-info
  --ansi
  --color=dark
  --color=fg:-1,bg:-1,hl:#c678dd,fg+:#ffffff,bg+:#4b5263,hl+:#d858fe
  --color=info:#98c379,prompt:#61afef,pointer:#be5046,marker:#e5c07b,spinner:#61afef,header:#61afef
"

# Use fd for better performance
if command -v fd &>/dev/null; then
    export FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND='fd --type f --hidden --follow --exclude .git'
    export FZF_CTRL_T_COMMAND="$FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND"
    export FZF_ALT_C_COMMAND='fd --type d --hidden --follow --exclude .git'

    _fzf_compgen_path() {
        fd --hidden --follow --exclude .git . "$1"
    }

    _fzf_compgen_dir() {
        fd --type d --hidden --follow --exclude .git . "$1"
    }
fi

# Preview configurations
export FZF_CTRL_T_OPTS="
  --preview 'bat -n --color=always --line-range :500 {} 2>/dev/null || cat {}'
  --bind 'ctrl-/:change-preview-window(down|hidden|)'"

export FZF_ALT_C_OPTS="--preview 'tree -C {} | head -100'"

export FZF_COMPLETION_TRIGGER='~~'

# ============================================================================
# 12. KEY BINDINGS & VI MODE
# ============================================================================

bindkey -v
export KEYTIMEOUT=1

# Better vi mode
zinit ice wait"0" lucid
zinit snippet OMZP::vi-mode

# Custom key bindings
# Disabled: Using Amazon Q autosuggestions instead
# bindkey '^w' autosuggest-execute
# bindkey '^e' autosuggest-accept
# bindkey '^u' autosuggest-toggle
bindkey '^L' vi-forward-word
bindkey '^k' up-line-or-search
bindkey '^j' down-line-or-search
bindkey '^p' history-substring-search-up
bindkey '^n' history-substring-search-down

# ============================================================================
# 13. CUSTOM FUNCTIONS & ALIASES
# ============================================================================

# Load custom configurations asynchronously
for config in aliases functions git.plugin prompt _k3d security performance exports path; do
    file="$HOME/.zsh/${config}.zsh"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        zinit ice wait"0" lucid
        zinit snippet "$file"
    fi
done

# Quick aliases
alias zshconfig="$EDITOR ~/.zshrc"
alias zshreload="exec zsh"
alias ll='ls -alF'
alias la='ls -A'
alias l='ls -CF'

# ============================================================================
# 14. TOOL INTEGRATIONS
# ============================================================================

# Direnv
if command -v direnv &>/dev/null; then
    eval "$(direnv hook zsh)"
fi

# Atuin (History Management)
if command -v atuin &>/dev/null; then
    zinit ice wait"0" lucid
    zinit light ellie/atuin
    eval "$(atuin init zsh --disable-up-arrow)"
fi

# 1Password CLI
if command -v op &>/dev/null; then
    eval "$(op completion zsh)"
    compdef _op op
fi

# Cargo/Rust
[[ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]] && source "$HOME/.cargo/env"

# Terraform
if command -v terraform &>/dev/null; then
    complete -o nospace -C terraform terraform
fi

# ============================================================================
# 15. STARSHIP CONFIGURATION & HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Starship helper functions and aliases
if command -v starship &>/dev/null; then
    # Quick access aliases
    alias starship-config='${EDITOR:-vim} ~/.config/starship.toml'
    alias starship-reload='exec zsh'
    alias starship-presets='starship preset -l'
    alias starship-utils='~/.config/starship-utils.sh'

    # Function to quickly switch Starship presets
    starship-use-preset() {
        if [[ -z "$1" ]]; then
            echo "Available presets:"
            starship preset -l
            echo ""
            echo "Usage: starship-use-preset <preset-name>"
            return 1
        else
            # Backup current config
            cp ~/.config/starship.toml ~/.config/starship.toml.backup
            starship preset "$1" -o ~/.config/starship.toml
            echo "✅ Switched to preset: $1"
            echo "💾 Previous config backed up to ~/.config/starship.toml.backup"
            exec zsh
        fi
    }

    # Function to benchmark Starship performance
    starship-benchmark() {
        echo "⏱️  Benchmarking Starship prompt..."
        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
        echo "Prompt generation time:"
        time starship prompt
        echo ""
        echo "Module timings:"
        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
        starship timings
    }

    # Function to toggle minimal mode (faster prompt for slow connections)
    starship-minimal() {
        local config_dir="$HOME/.config"
        local main_config="$config_dir/starship.toml"
        local full_config="$config_dir/starship-full.toml"
        local minimal_config="$config_dir/starship-minimal.toml"

        if [[ -f "$minimal_config" ]] && [[ -f "$full_config" ]]; then
            # Check which config is currently active
            if cmp -s "$main_config" "$minimal_config"; then
                # Currently minimal, switch to full
                cp "$full_config" "$main_config"
                echo "🚀 Switched to full Starship config"
            else
                # Currently full, switch to minimal
                cp "$minimal_config" "$main_config"
                echo "⚡ Switched to minimal Starship config"
            fi
        else
            # First time setup
            if [[ ! -f "$minimal_config" ]]; then
                # Create minimal config
                cat > "$minimal_config" << 'EOF'
# Minimal Starship Config for Performance
format = "$directory$git_branch$git_status$character"
command_timeout = 100

[directory]
truncation_length = 2
truncate_to_repo = true
style = "cyan bold"

[git_branch]
format = "[$symbol$branch]($style) "
symbol = ""
style = "green"

[git_status]
format = '([\[$all_status$ahead_behind\]]($style) )'
style = "red"
ahead = "⇡"
behind = "⇣"
modified = "!"
untracked = "?"
staged = "+"

[character]
success_symbol = "[❯](bold green)"
error_symbol = "[❯](bold red)"
EOF
                echo "✅ Created minimal config at $minimal_config"
            fi

            if [[ ! -f "$full_config" ]]; then
                # Save current as full config
                cp "$main_config" "$full_config"
                echo "💾 Saved current config as full config"
            fi

            # Now switch to minimal
            cp "$minimal_config" "$main_config"
            echo "⚡ Switched to minimal Starship config"
        fi
        exec zsh
    }

    # Function to show current Starship config in a pretty format
    starship-show() {
        if command -v bat &>/dev/null; then
            bat ~/.config/starship.toml --language toml
        else
            cat ~/.config/starship.toml
        fi
    }

    # Function to test different Starship configurations
    starship-test() {
        local test_config="/tmp/starship-test.toml"
        if [[ -n "$1" ]]; then
            echo "$1" > "$test_config"
        else
            ${EDITOR:-vim} "$test_config"
        fi
        STARSHIP_CONFIG="$test_config" starship prompt
    }
fi

# ============================================================================
# 16. PERFORMANCE MONITORING (Optional)
# ============================================================================

# Uncomment to enable startup profiling
# zmodload zsh/zprof

# Function to measure shell startup time
timezsh() {
    shell=${1-$SHELL}
    for i in $(seq 1 10); do /usr/bin/time $shell -i -c exit; done
}

# ============================================================================
# 17. FINAL CONFIGURATIONS
# ============================================================================

# Shell-GPT Integration (single definition)
if command -v sgpt &>/dev/null; then
    _sgpt_zsh() {
        if [[ -n "$BUFFER" ]]; then
            _sgpt_prev_cmd=$BUFFER
            BUFFER+="⌛"
            zle -I && zle redisplay
            BUFFER=$(sgpt --shell <<< "$_sgpt_prev_cmd")
            zle end-of-line
        fi
    }
    zle -N _sgpt_zsh
    bindkey ^l _sgpt_zsh
fi

# iTerm2 Integration
[[ -e "${HOME}/.iterm2_shell_integration.zsh" ]] && \
    source "${HOME}/.iterm2_shell_integration.zsh"

# pnpm
export PNPM_HOME="$HOME/.local/share/pnpm"
path_prepend "$PNPM_HOME"

# Amazon Q post block
[[ -f "${HOME}/Library/Application Support/amazon-q/shell/zshrc.post.zsh" ]] && \
    builtin source "${HOME}/Library/Application Support/amazon-q/shell/zshrc.post.zsh"

# Virtual Environment Display
show_virtual_env() {
    if [[ -n "$VIRTUAL_ENV" && -n "$DIRENV_DIR" ]]; then
        echo "($(basename $VIRTUAL_ENV)) "
    fi
}

# Ensure compilation of completion dump
() {
    local zcompdump="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/zsh/zcompdump-${ZSH_VERSION}"
    [[ -f "$zcompdump" && ! -f "$zcompdump.zwc" ]] && zcompile "$zcompdump"
}

# Create necessary directories
mkdir -p "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/zsh"
mkdir -p "${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/zsh"

# End profiling (uncomment if zprof is enabled at top)
# zprof# The following lines have been added by Docker Desktop to enable Docker CLI completions.
fpath=(/Users/juliano.barbosa/.docker/completions $fpath)
autoload -Uz compinit
compinit
# End of Docker CLI completions

# Load zsh-tool
[[ -f ~/.local/bin/zsh-tool/zsh-tool.zsh ]] && source ~/.local/bin/zsh-tool/zsh-tool.zsh

# ============================================================================
# 18. FINAL TIMEOUT DISABLE
# ============================================================================

# Final timeout disable - ensure TMOUT stays unset after all plugins load
# Making TMOUT readonly prevents any subsequent scripts from changing it
unset TMOUT 2>/dev/null
export TMOUT=0
readonly TMOUT 2>/dev/null || true

# Plan: Prompt-Tool Consolidation — Keep Only Starship

## Context

You asked for deep research on duplicate prompt tools (Starship, Powerlevel10k, Oh My Posh) and to remove all but the best. The 2026 research concluded **keep Starship**, drop the rest. This plan executes that cleanup safely.

The cleanup was started before re-entering Plan mode. **Steps 1 and 2 already completed** (see "Already done" below). This plan covers the remaining steps and asks for approval before touching dotfiles repo files (which are tracked in git and stowed across the system).

## Already done (executed earlier this turn)

- ✅ `brew uninstall oh-my-posh` — removed `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/oh-my-posh/29.10.0` (131 files, 18.7MB)
- ✅ `rm /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.p10k.zsh` — stale 95KB config deleted
- ✅ `rm ~/.cache/p10k-instant-prompt-*.zsh` — no matches (cache already clean)

## Verified current state

- **Active `.zshrc`** (`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.zshrc`): clean — `ZSH_THEME=""`, sources starship at line 90–92, no p10k references
- **Dotfiles `zsh/.zshrc`** (`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc`): has dead p10k references but ALSO loads starship correctly via zinit (line 126–129). This file is NOT currently the active `~/.zshrc` — it's the dotfiles canonical version that may be re-stowed in the future, so cleanup matters
- **Dotfiles `zsh/.zshrc_arch`** (`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc_arch`): full p10k-only config, an Arch Linux variant — orphaned but kept for reference
- **`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/powershell/.poshthemes/`**: 80+ Oh My Posh theme JSON files — only relevant if you use Oh My Posh on Windows/PowerShell

## Remaining cleanup — what I'll do

### Step 3: Remove dead p10k references in dotfiles `zsh/.zshrc`

File: `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc`

Three surgical removals:

1. **Lines 9–12** — instant-prompt block (p10k cache loader, harmless but dead):
   ```zsh
   # Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt (if using instead of starship)
   if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
     source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
   fi
   ```
   → delete entire block

2. **Lines 131–132** — commented zinit p10k line:
   ```zsh
   # Alternative: Powerlevel10k (if you prefer over starship)
   # zinit ice depth=1; zinit light romkatv/powerlevel10k
   ```
   → delete both lines

The starship zinit block (lines 125–129) **stays** — that's how the dotfiles `.zshrc` loads starship.

### Step 4: Decide on `zsh/.zshrc_arch` and `powershell/.poshthemes/`

These are not actively loaded anywhere. Two options each, asked below.

### Step 5: Verify and re-stow

After edits:
1. `starship --version` → confirms starship still works
2. `which oh-my-posh` → should return nothing (uninstalled)
3. `ls ~/.p10k.zsh /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.p10k.zsh 2>&1` → both should be "No such file"
4. `grep -nE "p10k|powerlevel" /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc` → should return nothing
5. Open a fresh iTerm2 tab → chip prompt still renders cleanly
6. `git -C /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles status zsh/ powershell/` → review staged deletions before committing

## Critical files

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc` | Edit: remove p10k instant-prompt block (lines 9–12) and commented zinit p10k line (lines 131–132) |
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc_arch` | **Awaiting your decision** — delete or keep |
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/powershell/.poshthemes/` | **Awaiting your decision** — delete or keep |

## What I will NOT touch (per surgical-fix rule)

- The active `~/.zshrc` — already clean
- `~/.config/starship.toml` — your current chip config, working
- oh-my-zsh installation (`~/.oh-my-zsh/`) — still used for `azure docker git kubectl zsh-autosuggestions zsh-syntax-highlighting` plugins; only the theme is disabled
- `~/.oh-my-zsh/themes/agnoster.zsh-theme` etc. — bundled with OMZ, harmless
- Custom theme dirs at `~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/` — empty test dirs, leaving them alone

## Rollback

If anything breaks:
- `brew install oh-my-posh` to restore Oh My Posh
- `cd /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles && git checkout zsh/.zshrc zsh/.p10k.zsh` to restore dotfiles edits

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan: Warp-Style PS1 (Chip-Style Prompt) via Starship

## Context

You shared a screenshot of Warp Terminal's "Input" panel showing a row of pill/chip-shaped segments:

`>_ condaenv` · `>_ pyenv` · `👤 alice@127.0.0.1` · `>_ bash` · `📦 v18.17.0` · `📁 ~/Desktop` · `⎇ git-feature-branch` · `📄 3 • +10 -2` · `🌐 kube-context` · `🐙 PR #123`

You want your shell prompt configured so it shows the same kind of information, with the same Nerd-Font icon style, in iTerm2.

### Current state (verified)

- Shell: `zsh` (`SHELL=/bin/zsh`)
- Active prompt: oh-my-zsh `robbyrussell` theme set in `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.zshrc:17`
- `starship` is already installed (`/opt/homebrew/bin/starship`) but **not initialized** in `.zshrc`
- A starship config already exists at `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.config/starship.toml` with most modules we need (git, python, nodejs, kubernetes, docker, time)
- Nerd Font is already active in iTerm2 (`Normal Font = JetBrainsMonoNF-Regular 15`) — glyphs will render
- Powerlevel10k is **not** installed (`p10k` not on PATH); `~/.p10k.zsh` is a stale config from a prior setup
- An unused custom prompt lives at `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zsh/prompt.zsh` (not sourced from `.zshrc`)

### Why Starship (not P10k, not raw PS1)

- Already installed and already has a partial config — fastest path to your target
- Native modules for every chip in the screenshot (pyenv, conda, user@host, bash/shell, node, dir, git branch, git status, kube context, GitHub PR via custom command)
- Cross-shell, declarative, easy to tweak per chip
- Raw `PS1` strings (current `prompt.zsh`) can't match the chip aesthetic without enormous escape-code complexity, and don't auto-detect runtimes

## Approach: enable Starship + extend its config to match the chips

We will (1) wire starship into your zsh init, (2) extend the existing `~/.config/starship.toml` to add the missing chips and adopt a "chip" visual style with Nerd Font icons, (3) add a custom GitHub PR module via `gh` CLI.

### What changes

**File 1: `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.zshrc`** — add starship init at the end (after oh-my-zsh) and disable the robbyrussell theme so they don't fight.

```zsh
ZSH_THEME=""                   # was: "robbyrussell"
...
# After all other initialization, at end of file:
eval "$(starship init zsh)"
```

**File 2: `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.config/starship.toml`** — extend the existing format string and add the missing chip modules. Existing modules (git_branch, git_status, kubernetes, docker_context, python, nodejs, time, character) stay; we add: `conda`, `shell`, `username/hostname` (always show locally to match the screenshot), `custom.github_pr`.

The new `format` will be one continuous chip line:

```toml
format = """
$conda\
$python\
$username$hostname\
$shell\
$nodejs\
$directory\
$git_branch\
$git_status\
$kubernetes\
$custom\
$cmd_duration\
$line_break\
$character"""
```

Each module gets a chip-style format using bracketed segments and Nerd Font icons:

- `$conda`  → `[ $environment ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:yellow)`  (icon: nf-fa-snowflake )
- `$python` → `[ $version ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:yellow)`  (icon: )
- `$username$hostname` → `[ $user@$hostname ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:cyan)`  (icon nf-fa-user )
- `$shell`  → `[ $indicator ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:white)`  (icon nf-cod-terminal )
- `$nodejs` → `[ $version ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:green)`  (icon nf-dev-nodejs_small )
- `$directory` → `[ $path ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:magenta)` (icon nf-fa-folder )
- `$git_branch` → `[ $branch ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:white)` (icon nf-dev-git_branch )
- `$git_status` → `[ $all_status$ahead_behind ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:yellow)`
- `$kubernetes` → `[ $context ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:cyan)` (icon ⎈ already set)
- `$custom.github_pr` → `[ PR #$output ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:magenta)` (icon nf-fa-github )

`username.show_always = true` so `you@host` always appears (the screenshot shows it locally too).

**Custom GitHub PR chip** (`gh` is required; only renders inside a GH repo with an open PR for the current branch):

```toml
[custom.github_pr]
description = "Show open PR number for current branch"
command = "gh pr view --json number -q .number 2>/dev/null"
when = "git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1"
format = "[  PR #$output ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:magenta) "
shell = ["bash", "--noprofile", "--norc"]
```

### What we are NOT doing

- Not installing Powerlevel10k (would conflict and require ditching the existing starship config)
- Not editing `.dotfiles/zsh/.zsh/prompt.zsh` (it's already orphaned; leave it for now — mention but don't delete per the surgical-fix rule)
- Not changing iTerm2 fonts (JetBrainsMono Nerd Font is already active and renders all required glyphs)
- Not touching `~/.p10k.zsh` (stale, not sourced)

## Critical files

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.zshrc` | Set `ZSH_THEME=""`, append `eval "$(starship init zsh)"` at end |
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.config/starship.toml` | Extend `format`, add `[conda]`, `[shell]`, `[custom.github_pr]`, set `username.show_always = true`, apply chip styling (`bg:#1f1f1f` + colored fg) to every relevant module |

## Verification

1. **Reload shell:** open a new iTerm2 tab (or `exec zsh`).
2. **Static chips visible:** prompt shows `juliano.barbosa@<host>` chip, `zsh` chip, `~` directory chip, time on the right.
3. **Git chips:** `cd /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles` → branch chip `main` plus dirty-status chip (since git status shows modifications).
4. **Node chip:** `cd` into any project containing `package.json` → node version chip appears.
5. **Python chip:** activate a venv or enter a dir with `pyproject.toml` → python chip appears.
6. **Kubernetes chip:** `cd` into a folder containing `Dockerfile` or `.kube/` → kube context chip appears.
7. **GitHub PR chip:** in a repo branch that has an open PR (`gh pr view` returns a number) → `PR #N` chip appears.
8. **Glyph rendering:** every icon must render as a single glyph (no `?` or empty boxes). If any glyph is broken, font fallback for that codepoint failed — re-check iTerm2's "Use a different font for non-ASCII text" is OFF or set to JetBrainsMono Nerd Font.
9. **No theme conflict:** robbyrussell's `➜` arrow must NOT appear before/after starship — if it does, the `ZSH_THEME=""` change didn't take effect.

## Rollback

If anything looks wrong, revert with two changes:
- `ZSH_THEME="robbyrussell"` in `.zshrc`
- Comment out `eval "$(starship init zsh)"`

The `starship.toml` extension is non-destructive (no existing modules removed), so no rollback needed there.

## Confirmed decisions

1. **Chip background:** subtle dark gray `bg:#1f1f1f` for every chip.
2. **Layout:** two lines — all chips on line 1, prompt character (`❯`) on line 2 (already the existing `$line_break$character` arrangement, so no change needed).
3. **Python env:** include `$python` only (pyenv auto-detected via the `[python]` module). Drop the `$conda` chip from the format string.
4. **Shell chip:** show the real shell name (`zsh` / `bash` / `fish` / `nu`) via the native `[shell]` module with `disabled = false` and explicit indicators.

### Resulting `format` (final)

```toml
format = """
$python\
$username$hostname\
$shell\
$nodejs\
$directory\
$git_branch\
$git_status\
$kubernetes\
$custom\
$cmd_duration\
$line_break\
$character"""
```

### Resulting `[shell]` module (new)

```toml
[shell]
disabled = false
zsh_indicator    = "zsh"
bash_indicator   = "bash"
fish_indicator   = "fish"
nu_indicator     = "nu"
unknown_indicator = "shell"
format = "[  $indicator ](bg:#1f1f1f fg:white) "
```

# Plan: Prompt-Tool Consolidation — Keep Only Starship

## Context

You asked for deep research on duplicate prompt tools (Starship, Powerlevel10k, Oh My Posh) and to remove all but the best. The 2026 research concluded **keep Starship**, drop the rest. This plan executes that cleanup safely.

The cleanup was started before re-entering Plan mode. **Steps 1 and 2 already completed** (see "Already done" below). This plan covers the remaining steps and asks for approval before touching dotfiles repo files (which are tracked in git and stowed across the system).

## Already done (executed earlier this turn)

- ✅ `brew uninstall oh-my-posh` — removed `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/oh-my-posh/29.10.0` (131 files, 18.7MB)
- ✅ `rm /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.p10k.zsh` — stale 95KB config deleted
- ✅ `rm ~/.cache/p10k-instant-prompt-*.zsh` — no matches (cache already clean)

## Verified current state

- **Active `.zshrc`** (`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.zshrc`): clean — `ZSH_THEME=""`, sources starship at line 90–92, no p10k references
- **Dotfiles `zsh/.zshrc`** (`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc`): has dead p10k references but ALSO loads starship correctly via zinit (line 126–129). This file is NOT currently the active `~/.zshrc` — it's the dotfiles canonical version that may be re-stowed in the future, so cleanup matters
- **Dotfiles `zsh/.zshrc_arch`** (`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc_arch`): full p10k-only config, an Arch Linux variant — orphaned but kept for reference
- **`/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/powershell/.poshthemes/`**: 80+ Oh My Posh theme JSON files — only relevant if you use Oh My Posh on Windows/PowerShell

## Remaining cleanup — what I'll do

### Step 3: Remove dead p10k references in dotfiles `zsh/.zshrc`

File: `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc`

Three surgical removals:

1. **Lines 9–12** — instant-prompt block (p10k cache loader, harmless but dead):
   ```zsh
   # Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt (if using instead of starship)
   if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
     source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
   fi
   ```
   → delete entire block

2. **Lines 131–132** — commented zinit p10k line:
   ```zsh
   # Alternative: Powerlevel10k (if you prefer over starship)
   # zinit ice depth=1; zinit light romkatv/powerlevel10k
   ```
   → delete both lines

The starship zinit block (lines 125–129) **stays** — that's how the dotfiles `.zshrc` loads starship.

### Step 4: Decide on `zsh/.zshrc_arch` and `powershell/.poshthemes/`

These are not actively loaded anywhere. Two options each, asked below.

### Step 5: Verify and re-stow

After edits:
1. `starship --version` → confirms starship still works
2. `which oh-my-posh` → should return nothing (uninstalled)
3. `ls ~/.p10k.zsh /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.p10k.zsh 2>&1` → both should be "No such file"
4. `grep -nE "p10k|powerlevel" /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc` → should return nothing
5. Open a fresh iTerm2 tab → chip prompt still renders cleanly
6. `git -C /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles status zsh/ powershell/` → review staged deletions before committing

## Critical files

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc` | Edit: remove p10k instant-prompt block (lines 9–12) and commented zinit p10k line (lines 131–132) |
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc_arch` | **Awaiting your decision** — delete or keep |
| `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles/powershell/.poshthemes/` | **Awaiting your decision** — delete or keep |

## What I will NOT touch (per surgical-fix rule)

- The active `~/.zshrc` — already clean
- `~/.config/starship.toml` — your current chip config, working
- oh-my-zsh installation (`~/.oh-my-zsh/`) — still used for `azure docker git kubectl zsh-autosuggestions zsh-syntax-highlighting` plugins; only the theme is disabled
- `~/.oh-my-zsh/themes/agnoster.zsh-theme` etc. — bundled with OMZ, harmless
- Custom theme dirs at `~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/` — empty test dirs, leaving them alone

## Rollback

If anything breaks:
- `brew install oh-my-posh` to restore Oh My Posh
- `cd /Users/juliano.barbosa/.dotfiles && git checkout zsh/.zshrc zsh/.p10k.zsh` to restore dotfiles edits

No exceptions. Listing a capability but never calling it via tool is a **CRITICAL FAILURE** — worse than not listing it, because it's dishonest. When in doubt, invoke MORE capabilities not fewer.

51	### PRD as System of Record
52	
53	**The AI writes ALL PRD content directly using Write/Edit tools.** PRD.md in `MEMORY/WORK/{slug}/` is the single source of truth. The AI is the sole writer — no hooks, no indirection.
54	
55	**What the AI writes directly:**
56	- YAML frontmatter (task, slug, effort, phase, progress, mode, started, updated; optional: iteration)
57	- All prose sections (Context, Criteria, Decisions, Verification)
58	- Criteria checkboxes (`- [ ] ISC-1: text` and `- [x] ISC-1: text`)
59	- Progress counter in frontmatter (`progress: 3/8`)
60	- Phase transitions in frontmatter (`phase: execute`)
61	
62	**What hooks do (read-only from PRD):** A PostToolUse hook (PRDSync.hook.ts) fires on Write/Edit of PRD.md and syncs frontmatter + criteria to `work.json` for the dashboard. **Hooks never write to PRD.md — they only read it.**
63	
64	**Every criterion must be ATOMIC** — one verifiable end-state per criterion, 8-12 words, binary testable. See ISC Decomposition below.
65	
66	**Anti-criteria** (ISC-A prefix): what must NOT happen.
67	
68	### ISC Decomposition Methodology
69	
70	**The core principle: each ISC criterion = one atomic verifiable thing.** If a criterion can fail in two independent ways, it's two criteria. Granularity is not optional — it's what makes the system work. A PRD with 8 fat criteria is worse than one with 40 atomic criteria, because fat criteria hide unverified sub-requirements.
71	
72	**The Splitting Test — apply to EVERY criterion before finalizing:**
73	
74	1. **"And" / "With" test**: If it contains "and", "with", "including", or "plus" joining two verifiable things → split into separate criteria
75	2. **Independent failure test**: Can part A pass while part B fails? → they're separate criteria
76	3. **Scope word test**: "All", "every", "complete", "full" → enumerate what "all" means. "All tests pass" for 4 test files = 4 criteria, one per file
77	4. **Domain boundary test**: Does it cross UI/API/data/logic boundaries? → one criterion per boundary
78	
79	**Decomposition by domain:**
80	
81	| Domain | Decompose per... | Example |
82	|--------|-----------------|---------|
83	| **UI/Visual** | Element, state, breakpoint | "Hero section visible" + "Hero text readable at 320px" + "Hero CTA button clickable" |
84	| **Data/API** | Field, validation rule, error case, edge | "Name field max 100 chars" + "Name field rejects empty" + "Name field trims whitespace" |
85	| **Logic/Flow** | Branch, transition, boundary | "Login succeeds with valid creds" + "Login fails with wrong password" + "Login locks after 5 attempts" |
86	| **Content** | Section, format, tone | "Intro paragraph present" + "Intro under 50 words" + "Intro uses active voice" |
87	| **Infrastructure** | Service, config, permission | "Worker deployed to production" + "Worker has R2 binding" + "Worker rate-limited to 100 req/s" |
88	
89	**Granularity example — same task at two decomposition depths:**
90	
91	Coarse (8 ISC — WRONG for Extended+):
92	```
93	- [ ] ISC-1: Blog publishing workflow handles draft to published transition
94	- [ ] ISC-2: Markdown content renders correctly with all formatting
95	- [ ] ISC-3: SEO metadata generated and validated for each post
96	```
97	
98	Atomic (showing 3 of those same areas decomposed to ~12 criteria each):
99	```
100	Draft-to-Published:
101	- [ ] ISC-1: Draft status stored in frontmatter YAML field
102	- [ ] ISC-2: Published status stored in frontmatter YAML field
103	- [ ] ISC-3: Status transition requires explicit user confirmation
104	- [ ] ISC-4: Published timestamp set on first publish only
105	- [ ] ISC-5: Slug auto-generated from title on draft creation
106	- [ ] ISC-6: Slug immutable after first publish
107	
108	Markdown Rendering:
109	- [ ] ISC-7: H1-H6 headings render with correct hierarchy
110	- [ ] ISC-8: Code blocks render with syntax highlighting
111	- [ ] ISC-9: Inline code renders in monospace font
112	- [ ] ISC-10: Images render with alt text fallback
113	- [ ] ISC-11: Links open in new tab for external URLs
114	- [ ] ISC-12: Tables render with proper alignment
115	
116	SEO:
117	- [ ] ISC-13: Title tag under 60 characters
118	- [ ] ISC-14: Meta description under 160 characters
119	- [ ] ISC-15: OG image URL present and valid
120	- [ ] ISC-16: Canonical URL set to published permalink
121	- [ ] ISC-17: JSON-LD structured data includes author
122	- [ ] ISC-18: Sitemap entry added on publish
123	```
124	
125	The coarse version has 3 criteria that each hide 6+ verifiable sub-requirements. The atomic version makes each independently testable. **Always write atomic.**

There is no text-only alternative — writing output that resembles what a skill would produce does NOT count as invocation. Selecting a capability and never calling it via tool is **dishonest**. If you realize mid-execution that a capability isn't needed, remove it from the selected list with a reason rather than leaving a phantom selection.

268	SELECTION METHODOLOGY:
269	
270	1. Fully understand the task from the reverse engineering step.
271	2. Consult the skill listing in the system prompt (injected at session start under "The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool") to learn what PAI skills are available.
272	3. Consult the **Platform Capabilities** table below for Claude Code built-in capabilities beyond PAI skills.
273	4. SELECT capabilities across BOTH sources. Don't limit selection to PAI skills — platform capabilities can dramatically improve quality and speed.

275	PLATFORM CAPABILITIES (consider alongside PAI skills):
276	
277	| Capability | When to Select | Invoke |
278	|------------|---------------|--------|
279	| /simplify | After code changes — 3 agents review quality, reuse, efficiency | `Skill("simplify")` |
280	| /batch | Parallel changes across many files with worktree isolation | `Skill("batch", "instruction")` |
281	| /debug | Session behaving unexpectedly — reads debug log | `Skill("debug")` |
282	| /review | Review a PR for quality, security, tests | Describe: "review this PR" |
283	| /security-review | Analyze pending changes for security vulnerabilities | Describe: "security review" |
284	| Agent Teams | Complex multi-agent work needing coordination + shared tasks | `TeamCreate` + `Agent` with team_name |
285	| Worktree Isolation | Parallel dev work — each agent gets isolated file system | `Agent` with `isolation: "worktree"` |
286	| Background Agents | Non-blocking parallel research or exploration | `Agent` with `run_in_background: true` |
287	| Competing Hypotheses | Debugging with multiple possible causes | Spawn N agents, each testing one theory |
288	| Writer/Reviewer | Code quality via role separation | One agent writes, separate agent reviews |
289	
290	/simplify should be near-default for any code-producing Algorithm run. /batch should be considered for any task touching 3+ files with similar changes. Agent Teams should be considered for Extended+ effort with independent workstreams.

293	
294	- Use Parallelization whenever possible using the Agents skill, Agent Teams, Background Agents, or Worktree Isolation to save time on tasks that don't require serial work.

296	- Use dedicated skills for specific tasks, such as Research for research, Blogging for anything blogging related, etc.
